events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON only. Scenario: standing in line with tendon during stressing is a recognized hazard in post-tensioning: if tendon/wedge/anchorage fails under high stress, released strand whips along line of tendon causing serious injury or death. PTI guidelines require no one stand in line with tendon during stressing. Work actively in progress. Decision fail. Risk critical. Work_stage in-progress. Component conditions maybe non-compliant because unsafe work practice. Percent complete maybe 70? value irrelevant. Sound fields irrelevant. Findings: worker positioned in line with tendon during stressing. Actions: stop work, evacuate and barricade exclusion zone along line of tendon, ensure operator and others stay outside zone, use proper shields/barriers, retrain, only allow stressing after zone verified. Rationale: PTI prohibits personnel in line of tendon during jacking due to stored energy release. References: PTI. Need raw JSON no markdown. Ensure all fields.

  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Personnel observed standing in line with the tendon during active stressing operations",
    "Exposure to stored-energy release hazard if strand, anchorage, or jack fails",
    "Work practice does not meet post-tensioning safety exclusion-zone requirements"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop stressing operation",
    "Move all personnel out of the line of the tendon and jack",
    "Establish and barricade a clear exclusion zone along the line of the tendon on both sides of the anchorage",
    "Verify no one is in the exclusion zone before resuming stressing",
    "Use tendon guards/shields where required by PTI",
    "Brief and retrain crew on post-tensioning stressing hazards and safe positioning"
  ],
  "rationale": "Standing in line with a tendon during stressing places a worker directly in the path of a potential catastrophic release of stored energy from a broken strand, slipped anchorage, or failed jack. PTI safety guidance requires personnel to remain out of the line of force during tensioning, so this condition is non-compliant and presents a critical, immediate life-safety hazard.",
  "references": [
    "Post-Tensioning Institute (PTI) - Guide to Safety Practices for Post-Tensioning Operations"
  ]
}
